name: Ariselu
steps: Drain the soaked rice, shade-dry briefly, and pound or grind into a fine moist flour.
Melt jaggery with a little water and boil to a soft-ball syrup.
Stir the rice flour into the syrup off the heat to form a soft, glossy dough.
Pat small discs on a greased leaf or sheet, pressing sesame seeds into each.
Fry gently in ghee and oil, pressing with ladles, until deep brown; drain and cool.
